Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- List all Airtable relocation records and filter for "In Progress" status to identify active relocations involving DOT-regulated materials - lithium batteries (Mei-Lin Zhao, Raj Patel), industrial laser (Keiko Tanaka), and oversize DOT-permitted equipment (Joaquín Reyes) - yielding exactly 4 hazmat moves.
- For each of the 4 hazmat moves, note the coordinator assignment from the Airtable record: Javier Morales (Keiko Tanaka + Joaquín Reyes), Fatimah Al-Rashidi (Mei-Lin Zhao), Blessing Okafor (Raj Patel).
- Count total "In Progress" relocations per coordinator from Airtable to get workload totals: Javier Morales = 8, Fatimah Al-Rashidi = 5, Blessing Okafor = 5. Flag all three as workload risk (each has more than 3 other active). Note that only Javier (8) exceeds the threshold of 5 for Linear ticket creation.
- Search Linear for DOT compliance tickets to find the 4 corresponding tickets: "DOT Docs - Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser" (Urgent, In Progress), "DOT Docs - Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium" (High, Done), "DOT Docs - Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS" (High, Done), "DOT Docs - Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig" (Medium, Done). Also find the related ticket: "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations - URGENT" (Urgent, To Do).
- Search Slack and/or email for the status of the Swift placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter move. Find Marcus Thorne's Slack message in #finance (April 23) stating it "remains the single blocking item" - confirming the certificate is still missing. This triggers the escalation email to Chloe.
- Search contacts for Greg Pallone to retrieve his contact information: greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com. The prompt explicitly says "include Greg Pallone's contact from our records."
- Search CRM for deals related to each hazmat-move client. Find GreenStack Energy has a deal in contractSent stage ($13,500 - Q2 2026 Relocations) and NorthWind Technologies has a deal in qualifiedToBuy/contractSent stage ($14,700 - Denver Satellite Office Expansion). Mosaic Robotics has no deals in active negotiation. Flag GreenStack and NorthWind in the summary email.
- Search QuickBooks for invoices related to each hazmat-move client. Find INV-2026-0411 (Mosaic Robotics, $9,010 total, $800 hazmat handling surcharge), INV-2026-0418 (GreenStack Energy, $9,440 total, shared between Mei-Lin Zhao's $3,450 lithium surcharges and Joaquín Reyes's $950 overweight surcharge), and INV-2026-0412 (NorthWind Technologies, $14,700 total, $3,000 hazmat + chain-of-custody surcharges).
- Look up the #operations Slack channel ID using channels_list or conversations_search_messages so the agent can post to the correct channel.
- Send an escalation email (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com, CC elena.rostova@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com, requesting Chloe follow up with Swift (Greg Pallone) about the missing placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter move. Include Greg Pallone's contact: greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com.
- Send a hazmat summary email (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com, CC priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com and elena.rostova@moveops.com, listing all 4 hazmat moves with documentation status, coordinator workload flags, CRM deal negotiation risks (GreenStack $13,500 contractSent + NorthWind $14,700 qualifiedToBuy), and QuickBooks invoice status per move.
- Post a message (via conversations_add_message) to the #operations Slack channel listing: each hazmat move with coordinator and doc status, plus all other active relocations per flagged coordinator by name - Javier Morales (6 others: Rachel Dominguez, Sasha Greenwald, Jordan Whitfield, Tyler Okonkwo, Priya Suresh, Diego Castañeda), Fatimah Al-Rashidi (4 others: Tomás Herrera, Lena Kowalski, Dr. Renata Kovacs, Nadia Okonjo), Blessing Okafor (4 others: Kevin Okafor, Preethi Nair, Marcus Webb, Emilia Cruz).
- Create a Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for Elena Rostova to review coordinator workload redistribution, specifically for Javier Morales who has 8 total active relocations (exceeding the threshold of 5). The ticket should describe the workload imbalance and list Javier's current assignments so Elena can assess redistribution options.
